calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve The Proceeds Of Sale Of Securities Petition For Final Discharge And Order For Discharge Of Executors
Off Calendar Feb-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin for Marisa Petri and Enzo Malanco.

The Court questions why the Personal Representatives didn't proceed with distribution according to the order for final distribution and questions what the entry for $18,780.51 titled "adjustment to accounting" is for. Mr. Lopin stated that the order was very specific as to distribution in kind, that the Clients did not follow the order, that his Clients did not inform him and that they just went ahead and sold the securities. Mr. Lopin also apologized to the Court and stated that he does not have information for that entry in front of him. The Petition to Approve the Proceeds of Sale of Securities, etc. is under submission. If Counsel can get a declaration regarding the $18,780.51 entry in quickly, the Court will look into it.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition To Approve The Proceeds Of Sale Of Securities Petition For Final Discharge And Order For Discharge Of Executors

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A New Petition
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Off Calendar

Petition To Amend The Order On Petition For Final Discharge And Order For Discharge Of Executors
Off Calendar Jul-30-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin for Marisa Petri and Enzo Malanca.

The Court noted that the intent of the parties is not to seek that the final order be amended, but to seek some level of approval of what has been done and that it requires an amended petition. Mr. Lopin stated that his Clients were ordered to distribute the securities in kind, that they did not do so, that they sold the securities and that the purpose of the declaration is to give the Court a time line and show that there is no money in the account and that all of the money has been accounted for. Mr. Lopin also expressed frustration in having to file another petition, spoke of his Clients being elderly, questions how what he is being asked to do is good for the estate and for who, and spoke of withdrawing this petition. The Court reminds Mr. Lopin that his Clients disobeyed a Court order, that his Clients are required to account to the Court and anyone entitled to notice as to how they proceeded, that he filed a petition seeking relief for something that is not available and that he is required to file something that is appropriate following the rules and laws that govern administration of an estate. Mr. Lopin withdraws his petition. The Petition to Amend the Order, etc. is off calendar. Status on filing of an appropriate petition that seeks appropriate relief to deal with where they now stand is set for September 24, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lopin is to have his petition filed by September 17, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Amend The Order On Petition For Final Discharge And Order For Discharge Of Executors
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Continued To Jul-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Loren Lopin for Marisa Petri and Enzo Malanca.

Mr. Lopin stated that he is not clear as to what the Court wants and that he has addressed to the Probate Examiner's notes. The Court noted that the Examiner's notes from May 21st had not been addressed, that the tentative ruling explained what was needed and stated that the issue is the petition as filed does not clearly state what order it seeks to modify and it does not provide what the Court's legal authority is to modify an order for final discharge. The Petition to Amend the Order on Petition for Final Discharge, etc. is continued to July 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Mr. Lopin to respond to Examiner's notes no later than July 23, 2019.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
